victim.value: Barry, the building janitor who has been sweeping dust for eleven years
victim.quote: I can't believe he's... he's gone.

closed_world.value: A building with a square room and mysterious locked black door
closed_world.quote: A wide black door taunts me from the other end of the room.

investigator.value: Barry, the building janitor experiencing events from first person perspective
investigator.quote: My name's Barry... I sweep this building every morning.

red_herring.value: The locked black door with crimson stains suggests murder scene, but reveals George who makes dust legitimately
red_herring.quote: The crimson stains near the handle only heighten my curiosity. I'll bet that room has something to do with the floor

clue_fairness.value: Daily dust accumulation pattern and crimson door stains presented to reader before Barry investigates
clue_fairness.quote: I arrive every morning to the floor of this square room completely covered with dust.

social_world.value: Employment hierarchy between Barry (custodian) and mysterious building operations he's excluded from knowing
social_world.quote: Eleven years on the job has taught me two things. The first, and most important, don't ask questions.

reveal_mechanic.value: Supernatural revelation through death experience - Barry realizes he has died and the mystery was his own fate
reveal_mechanic.quote: I can't believe he's... he's gone. My guess is assured at that statement. I've died.

investigator_wound.value: Barry's compulsive need to understand his mundane work environment despite being told never to ask questions
investigator_wound.quote: don't ask questions. This is also the easiest rule to follow... but I regain my composure as fury burns up my chest

_meta.title: ebook austin c drake dream of the dead
